questions.question: Which of the following best describes the competencies required by project managers in order to achieve effective project management?
questions.explanation: Project managers need three types of competence: knowledge (what they know), performance (what they can do), and personal (how they behave).

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT considered a stakeholder of a project?
questions.explanation: Stakeholders are people who affect or are affected by the project. Rivals are competitors outside the project, not stakeholders.

questions.question: Disagreement on issues concerning the project through which parties involved perceive it as a threat to their needs, interests or concerns are referred to as project ______.
questions.explanation: When people see project issues as threats to what they need or care about, that creates project conflict.
questions.reference: Unit 4, Session 3 – Conflict in Project Teams

questions.question: Project vision conflict arises when two different groups of workers do not work efficiently due to their differences.
questions.explanation: True. Vision conflicts happen when people have different goals or values, which makes them work less efficiently together.

questions.question: An approach to conflict that involves a party to either physically or psychologically remove himself/herself from the conflict is referred to as ______.
questions.explanation: Avoidance means walking away from the conflict, either by leaving physically or just mentally checking out.

questions.question: Obtaining information from prospective sellers on how projects needs can be met is referred to as ______.
questions.explanation: Solicitation means asking potential sellers for bids, quotes, or proposals on how they can meet your project needs.

questions.question: Which of the following factors is said to influence demand fluctuation?
questions.explanation: Demand changes because of seasonality, taxes, price changes, and availability. Seasonality is a main cause.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the consequences one is likely to experience when project scope is incompletely defined?
options: Leads to schedule slips and cost overruns
Leads to unnecessary work and confusion
Results in misrepresentation in requirements and design
questions.explanation: When scope is not fully defined, you get wrong estimates and rework, which cause schedule delays and cost overruns.

questions.question: Ambiguity in scope management often results in ______.
questions.explanation: When scope is unclear or vague, people get confused and end up doing work that is not needed.

questions.question: Being transient in project scope definition often results in ______.
questions.explanation: Transient means always changing. When scope keeps changing, it leads to scope creep where the project grows bigger than planned.
questions.reference: Unit 3, Session 3 – Scope Control and Scope Creep

questions.question:
Which of the following statement(s) best describes the nature of a project?
I. It is commenced and never completed.
II. The duration is short.
III. The outcome created by the project is automatically short.
IV. Outcome is unique.
questions.explanation: I is false (projects end). II is false (projects can be long). III is false (outcomes can last forever). Only IV is correct because every project creates something unique.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 2 – Nature and Characteristics of Projects

questions.question: Which of the following circumstances will mark the end of a project?
options: Termination because objectives cannot be met
When a new product is developed
When safety and risk controls are addressed
When the project is considered a temporal one
questions.explanation: A project ends when objectives are achieved OR when it is terminated because objectives cannot be met.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 2 – Project Closure and Termination

questions.question: The following are considered as importance of a project, except ______.
options: making activities in the project shorter than expected
questions.explanation: Projects help handle complex products, speed up work, and satisfy shareholders. Making economic periods longer is not a benefit of projects.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 3 – Importance of Project Management

questions.question: Which of the following activities is the last step to undertake in managing projects?
questions.explanation: First you find requirements, then address stakeholder needs, then set up communication. The final step is balancing constraints like time, cost, and scope.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 6 – Nature of Projects and Project Management

questions.question: The three most important traditional constraints of project management referred to as the triple constraints consists of ______.
questions.explanation: The triple constraints are Scope, Time (Schedule), and Cost (Budget).

questions.question: Which of the following is a key feature of the second phase of the project life cycle?
questions.explanation: The second phase is Planning. In planning, you outline all the activities that need to be done.

questions.question: Project termination type where the needed resources to execute the projects are either lacking or inadequately available is referred to as ______.
questions.explanation: Starvation happens when resources are slowly taken away until the project cannot continue and dies.

questions.question: Advertising, shipping and utilities are examples of project overhead cost.
questions.explanation: True. These are indirect costs that support the project but cannot be traced to one specific task.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 2 – Indirect and Overhead Costs

questions.question: Total labour cost of a project is realized by ______.
options: adding all cost associated with labour
dividing overhead cost with direct labour cost
multiplying direct labour rate by total labour hours
multiplying salaries with total labour hours
questions.explanation: Labour cost is calculated by multiplying the hourly rate by the total number of hours worked.

questions.question:
Which of the following are examples of short-term project funding?
I. Bank overdrafts
II. Loans
III. Sale and leaseback
IV. Retained profit
questions.explanation: Only bank overdraft is short-term. Loans, sale and leaseback, and retained profits are long-term funding sources.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 4 – Sources of Project Finance
